USA.name: United States
USA.infoText: The United States' 2020 population density concentrated along the coasts, particularly the Northeast megalopolis, California, and Florida. Major metropolitan areas formed dense clusters, while the Great Plains, Rocky Mountains, and Alaska maintained very low densities, reflecting both geography and economic activity patterns.

CHN.name: China
CHN.infoText: China's 2020 population density revealed a dramatic east-west divide. The eastern half, especially the Yangtze and Pearl River deltas, was densely populated with major urban centers. The western regions, including the Tibetan Plateau and Xinjiang, remained sparsely populated due to harsh terrain and climate.

VEN.name: Venezuela
VEN.infoText: Venezuela's 2020 population density was highly concentrated in the northern coastal regions, particularly around Caracas and other major cities. The southern rainforests and interior llanos remained sparsely populated due to dense vegetation and limited infrastructure.

GBR.name: Britain
GBR.infoText: Britain's 2020 population density showed extreme concentration in London and the Southeast, forming one of the world's largest metropolitan areas. Northern England, Scotland, and Wales remained significantly less dense, reflecting historical industrialization patterns and geographic constraints.

DEU.name: Germany
DEU.infoText: Germany's 2020 population density showed clear concentration in urban corridors, particularly along the Rhine and in major cities like Berlin, Hamburg, and Munich. Rural areas in the east and parts of Bavaria remained less densely populated.

FRA.name: France
FRA.infoText: France's 2020 population density was heavily concentrated in Paris and the northern urban belt, forming one of Europe's largest metropolitan regions. Southern France, the Massif Central, and rural areas remained significantly less populated.

IND.name: India
IND.infoText: India's 2020 population density revealed massive concentration in the Indo-Gangetic Plain, one of the world's most densely populated regions. The Thar Desert, Himalayan foothills, and northeastern states remained relatively sparse due to terrain and climate constraints.

RUS.name: Russia
RUS.infoText: Russia's 2020 population density showed extreme concentration in European Russia, particularly around Moscow and major cities. Siberia and the Far East, covering most of the country's territory, remained extremely sparsely populated due to harsh climate and vast distances.

AUS.name: Australia
AUS.infoText: Australia's 2020 population density showed extreme concentration along the eastern and southeastern coasts, particularly around Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane. The vast interior, including the Outback and desert regions, remained virtually uninhabited.

CAN.name: Canada
CAN.infoText: Canada's 2020 population density was heavily concentrated in the southernmost regions, particularly the Quebec City-Windsor corridor and southern Ontario. Northern Canada, including the tundra and Arctic regions, remained virtually empty due to extreme climate.

AFG.name: Afghanistan
AFG.infoText: Afghanistan's 2020 population density was highly uneven. While the national average was low, population concentrated heavily in a few areas like Kabul. In contrast, southwestern deserts and central highlands remained very sparsely populated, largely due to geography and water access.

ALB.name: Albania
ALB.infoText: Albania's 2020 population density showed a clear coastal concentration. The western lowlands, especially around Tirana and Durrës, hosted most of the population. Meanwhile, the eastern mountainous regions remained sparsely populated, reflecting the country's rugged topography.

DZA.name: Algeria
DZA.infoText: Algeria's 2020 population density was concentrated in the northern coastal regions and the capital Algiers. The vast Sahara Desert in the south remained virtually uninhabited, creating a stark contrast between Mediterranean coastline and desert interior.

AGO.name: Angola
AGO.infoText: Angola's 2020 population density was concentrated along the coast and in the capital Luanda. The interior highlands and eastern regions remained sparsely populated. The country's oil-rich coast contrasted sharply with underdeveloped inland areas.

ARM.name: Armenia
ARM.infoText: Armenia's 2020 population density showed concentration in the Aras River valley and around Yerevan. The mountainous terrain, particularly the Lesser Caucasus range, limited settlement in eastern and western regions.

GRC.name: Greece
GRC.infoText: Greece's 2020 population density was concentrated in Athens and other major urban centers. The numerous islands and mountainous mainland, particularly the Pindus mountains, remained less densely populated.

ITA.name: Italy
ITA.infoText: Italy's 2020 population density showed extreme concentration in the Po Valley, particularly around Milan and Turin. Southern Italy, Sardinia, and Sicily were less densely populated, while the Alps and Apennines created natural barriers to settlement.

MNG.name: Mongolia
MNG.infoText: Mongolia's 2020 population density was extremely low across the entire country. The capital Ulaanbaatar and surrounding areas held the majority of the population, while the vast steppe and Gobi Desert regions remained virtually uninhabited.

POL.name: Poland
POL.infoText: Poland's 2020 population density showed concentration in urban centers, particularly Warsaw, Kraków, and the Silesian industrial region. Rural areas in the east and northeast remained less densely populated.

SSD.name: South Sudan
SSD.infoText: South Sudan's 2020 population density was extremely low, with concentration in the capital Juba and along major rivers. The vast swamps of the Sudd and remote areas remained virtually uninhabited.
